text: Selective modification of nitrogen-containing heteroaromatics is a fundamental theme in chemical biology and drug discovery. Although recent progress has demonstrated the feasibility of increasing or decreasing the size of a heteroaromatic core, direct atom exchange at the core nitrogen atom remains rare. Here, we demonstrate a $^{14}$N $\rightarrow$ $^{15}$N isotopic exchange process in various heteroaromatic nitrogen-containing compounds through a skeletal editing protocol. The protocol involves initial $N$-triflylation of the heteroaromatic starting material, followed by ring opening and subsequent core rearrangement that triggers the $^{14}$N $\rightarrow$ $^{15}$N exchange process catalyzed by $^{15}$N-aspartate. The $^{15}$N-succinyl key intermediate derived from the $^{14}$N $\rightarrow$ $^{15}$N exchange process undergoes an efficient decarboxylation process to yield the desired $^{15}$N-enriched heteroaromatic compounds. Overall, the protocol allows the efficient production of multiple $^{15}$N-enriched pyridine and pyrimidines at the desired position in quantitative yields and with quantitative $^{15}$N atom recovery, thus bypassing complex multistep syntheses for isotopic substitution.
